message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>We investigated the presence of Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) RNA at different treatment stages of 15 wastewater treatment plants (WWTPs) in two North Indian states of Rajasthan and Uttarakhand. Untreated (influent), biologically treated, and disinfected wastewater samples were collected from May to August 2020. The qualitative analysis of the wastewater for the presence of SARS-CoV-2 RNA was done using different pre-processing methods. SARS-CoV-2 RNA was detected in 11 out of 39 wastewater samples in Jaipur district and 5 out of 17 wastewater samples in Haridwar District using Reverse-Transcriptase Quantitative Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-qPCR) for qualitative detection. None of the 56 samples tested for post-secondary or tertiary treatment were found positive for SARS-CoV-2 RNA. The findings indicate that there are no SARS-CoV-2 related risks involved with using the treated effluent for non-potable applications. In contrast, untreated wastewater may be a potential route of viral transmission to the WWTP and sanitation workers. Future studies are imperative to understand the survival rates of these viruses in wastewater.</jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Graphical Abstract</jats:title>
                  <jats:fig id="ufig1" position="float" fig-type="figure" orientation="portrait">
                    <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="20197178v1_ufig1" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                  </jats:fig>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Mild to moderate genome load observed in the municipal wastewater samples.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        Increased patient numbers post-lockdown correspond to a decrease in the C
                        <jats:sub>T</jats:sub>
                        value of genes.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Presence of SARS-CoV-2 genome load was observed in untreated wastewater.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>E gene was present in abundance in wastewaters as compared to the N gene and RdRp gene.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>SARS-CoV-2 genome load was absent in secondary and tertiary treated effluent.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Detection of SARS-CoV-2 RNA in fourteen wastewater treatment systems in Uttarakhand and Rajasthan States of North India
